lines.text: BROOKLYN 1, N. Y.
Latest reports listed
analyzed in Flying [unclear]
Review, printed Since Au[unclear]
April 6 1954
Federal Bureau of Investigation
Pennsylvania and 9th
Washington DC
Subject: Possible subversive ri[unclear]
Gentlemen:
Further to my registered letter 33520 of March 1, listing persons wo[unclear]
planned contacting for the new CIVILIAN SAUCER INTELLIGENCE, to discuss f[unclear]
Saucers, I am sending a second report on membership. I have reached much
definite impressions since then, on some members, and enclose these for w[unclear]
they are worth.
From her actions, general attitudes, and methods, I am almost positiv[e]
[REDACTED] is part of some other group, possibly subversive, investig[ating]
the field for possible civilian information on the subject.
From her attitude, toward [REDACTED] and [REDACTED] her contacting me t[unclear]
after I had written [REDACTED] twice after long activity, his unusual will[ingness]
to cooperate with our group, different from his previous closemouthed att[itude]
I am almost positive he is part of the same group, and either directs her
the entire group in New York City. He does a great deal of travelling fr[om]
New York to Washington and Baltimore, and may be some sort of courier.
A clipping has come in that [REDACTED] also previously listed,
states he is a research 'Assistante Professor' to PROFESSOR [REDACTED]
in Albuquerque, now studying 'Meteoritics'. I have nothing as to Profes[sor]
[REDACTED] background other than he has given out some peculiarly mis-infor[med]
ideas on the subject in recent years.
These include: a widely circulated release that the Green fireballs we[re]
not 'natural' phenomena, and probably were American secret weapons: an a[rticle]
in People Today that saucers and fireballs were probably coming from nort[hern]
Russian bases: a news release that flying saucers over northern Japan i[n]
Jan. 1953, were probably Russian guided missiles, contrary to all known
performances of missiles.
After sending [REDACTED] some special information on saucer rep[orts]
he mentioned after long delay that he had been at Cambridge, Mass. Whethe[r]
or not there is a connection, PROFESSOR [REDACTED], who wrote a delib[erately]
misinformative and debunking book on flying saucers, lives near Cambridge
while working at Harvard. According to some persons who have supposedly w[orked]
his daughter, they are unquestionably communists or fellow travellers. Ho[wever]
I am not sure of the character of the persons who [unclear] and must stat[e]
is only hearsay evidence.
